question.content: As shown in the figure, in the isosceles triangle ABC, AB = BC. Let I be the incenter, M be the midpoint of BI, and P be a point on side AC satisfying AP = 3PC. A point H on the extension of PI satisfies MH perpendicular PH. Let Q be the midpoint of the minor arc AB on the circumcircle of triangle ABC. Find the degree of angle BHQ. Answer format requirement: <expr>. Strictly forbidden: any extra text beyond this format.

step_1.content: Let N be the midpoint of AC. Since AP = 3PC, point P is the midpoint of NC. In the isosceles triangle, B, I, N are collinear and CN is perpendicular to BI. Because Q is the midpoint of the minor arc AB, points C, I, Q are collinear and QI = QB.
step_1.key_point: Convert the given ratio and arc-midpoint condition into midpoint and perpendicular facts.

step_3.content: As M is the midpoint of BI and QI = QB, line QM is the perpendicular bisector of BI, so QM is perpendicular to BI. Since CN is also perpendicular to BI, we get QM parallel to CN.
step_3.key_point: The key structural relation is QM parallel to CN.

step_4.content: Compare triangles HMQ and HIB. Because H, I, P are collinear and MH is perpendicular to PH, angle HMQ equals angle HIB. Also angle IHM equals angle INP equals 90 degrees, and HM/HI = NP/NI = MQ/IB from P being the midpoint of NC together with QM parallel to CN.
step_4.key_point: These angle and ratio relations give triangle HMQ similar to triangle HIB.

step_6.content: From the similarity, angle HQM equals angle HBI, so H, M, B, Q are concyclic. Since B, M, I are collinear and QM is perpendicular to BI, angle BMQ = 90 degrees. Therefore angle BHQ = 90 degrees.
step_6.key_point: The cyclic quadrilateral transfers the right angle at M to the target angle at H.
